summary: Blues‑heavy early set with standout Heads Up and Midnight Hour
blurb: The show delivers a straightforward blues‑R&B set. Highlights include powerful renditions of Heads Up, Stormy Monday Jam, and Midnight Hour, while Bob’s vocals slip slightly on I Know You Rider. The soundboard capture is clear but Jerry sits forward in the mix.
review: Recorded on March 19 1966 at Carthay Studios during the Pico Acid Test, this performance captures the Grateful Dead in their formative blues phase. The setlist leans heavily into R&B classics – Viola Lee Blues, One Kind Favor and a string of soulful numbers that showcase the band’s nascent chemistry. Standout moments arrive with Jerry’s blistering lead on Heads Up, a gritty Stormy Monday Jam, and a spirited take on Midnight Hour; these tracks illustrate the group’s deep‑rooted love for the blues. Bob’s vocal delivery is occasionally off‑key (notably on I Know You Rider), but Phil holds down a solid bass foundation while Bill provides steady drumming. The soundboard source offers crisp separation and an upfront guitar presence, making it the most listenable version despite minor tonal imbalances.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: One recording is clearly positive, highlighting blues‑heavy work and standout songs; the second recording offers a mixed but neutral view with no strong criticism of performance. The overall tone across analyses leans positive, justifying a modest sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear stereo separation and good capture of the standout blues performances.

key_highlights: Blues‑heavy set with strong Heads Up and Midnight Hour performances.
Stormy Monday Jam showcases early jam chemistry.
Bob’s vocals slip slightly on I Know You Rider.
Recording offers crisp separation but Jerry sits forward in the mix.

band_performance.Jerry: Prominent, blues‑heavy guitar work praised on Heads Up, Stormy Monday Jam and Midnight Hour; sits a bit forward in the mix.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals are slightly off‑key on a couple of tracks, especially I Know You Rider.
